claims.statement: A Change cannot reach Accepted without current Evidence, Knowledge Closure, scope analysis, and an authorized Decision.
Failed, stale, incomplete, forged, or worker-self-reported Evidence cannot satisfy a Verification Obligation.
An Accepted Change Package is content-addressed and becomes invalid when its bound repository or package content changes.

For each Change write lifecycle operation, Change Kernel compiles one Project Model-owned opaque path ownership product from one stabilized exact Model and tracked-path observation, passes it process-locally to Context Capsule and touched or prospective path enforcement, rejects frozen/current ownership drift, and never interprets selectors or dispositions itself.
Integration requires untampered full-Gate assurance bound to the current Accepted Change Package.

The Change Kernel emits an Architecture Acceptance Profile only after a bounded stabilization check observes two consecutive identical composite digests over Project Model, Git content, and Change Store sources; it classifies Evidence as current only when the Evidence's Project Model, Git content, and verification-subject bindings match that stabilized source set, while sealed historical and invalid Evidence remain separate.

Change Kernel selects each Architecture Profile record window from one stabilized composite Project Model, Git, and Change Store snapshot in canonical Change-id order before Profile facts or frozen-baseline route products are compiled; it enforces fixed per-window record, fact, provider-work, route-byte, and output-byte ceilings and emits an authenticated opaque continuation bound to the exact Kernel process, snapshot digest, query-shape digest, ordering policy, and last emitted Change. On an unchanged snapshot, successive Kernel-issued continuations visit every Change exactly once, while malformed, modified, expired, unissued, cross-process, cross-snapshot, or cross-query continuations fail closed without compiling facts or historical providers outside the selected window.

The fixed Change Kernel Profile-window proof rejects full-history materialization before selection, page-external fact or Provider work, omitted or duplicate unchanged-snapshot traversal, GET-time Store writes, output beyond hard ceilings, and malformed, modified, expired, unissued, cross-process, cross-snapshot, or cross-query continuations.

The Change Kernel compiles one bounded Workbench semantic projection from a stabilized composite Project Model, Git, and Change Store snapshot: authoring options contain the complete canonical set of exact Claims visible through each Module's owned or dependency Contracts and every acceptance-eligible Gate and command route projected for them by the same current Project Model route product as the Architecture Profile; existing-Change compile, Gate, and accept actions contain the complete canonical set permitted by the frozen Governance Baseline and Kernel lifecycle policy; their exact Claim-route annotations derive from the compiler-owned Verification Plan; and every unavailable action carries a non-empty duplicate-free disabled-reason code list containing only workbenchDisabledReasonCodes in workbenchDisabledReasonPrecedence order.

The Change Kernel obtains Workbench authoring Claim visibility and acceptance-eligible current or frozen-Plan routes only through Project Model's bounded module Claim/Gate projection over each corresponding opaque route product. It does not traverse Contract publicContracts or dependency via relations, interpret Gate or command scope, or filter route effectiveModuleRefs, while retaining ownership only of stable source orchestration, lifecycle action policy, disabled-reason ordering, and compiler-bound Verification Plan validation.
